info.name: Ekko API
info.description:
API collection for Ekko — AI-Powered Voice Assistant Platform.

Covers:
- REST health and stream control endpoints
- GraphQL queries, mutations, and subscriptions
- Automated test scripts with response validation

## Authentication
Local-only app: auto-authenticates as `dev-user`. No tokens required.

## Error Responses
All errors follow:
```json
{
  "detail": "Human-readable message"
}
```

exec: // Generates a unique X-Request-ID for request correlation/tracing

// Validate Content-Type header (skip for OPTIONS preflight)
// No server error leakage in non-5xx responses
pm.test('No internal error details leaked', function () {

item.name: Health
item.description: System health and readiness checks. Use these to verify the server is running and all dependencies are operational.

request.description:
Lightweight health check reporting TCP audio servers and queue status.

Returns:
- `ok`: Overall system health boolean
- `details.sys_server_listening`: System audio TCP server status
- `details.mic_server_listening`: Microphone TCP server status
- `details.transcripts_queue_present`: Transcript processing queue availability
- `details.duckdb_enabled`: DuckDB analytics engine status
- `details.duckdb_sqlite_probe`: (conditional) SQLite attachment test result

exec: pm.test('Health data structure is valid', function () {

request.description: Basic GraphQL health check returning service status and environment info.

exec: pm.test('Deep health check has dependencies', function () {

request.description: Deep health check with dependency probes (database, DuckDB). Use this to verify all external services are connected and operational.

item.name: Stream Control
item.description: Audio stream lifecycle management. Controls real-time audio capture from system/microphone sources.

exec: pm.test('Response schema is valid StreamResponse', function () {

request.description:
Start audio streaming. Initiates device check and begins capturing audio from configured sources.

Preconditions:
- Audio device must be available
- Server must be in idle or paused state

Possible errors:
- 503: Audio device not available

exec: pm.test('Response schema is valid StreamResponse', function () {

request.description:
Pause audio streaming. Suspends audio capture without releasing device resources.

The stream can be resumed with start_stream.
Start audio stream via GraphQL mutation.
Pause audio stream via GraphQL mutation.

item.name: Conversations
item.description: Conversation lifecycle management. Create, query, message, and end AI-powered voice conversations.

exec: pm.test('Conversation created with valid structure', function () {
// Store conversation ID for subsequent requests

request.description:
Start a new conversation session. Optionally attach metadata for tracking.

The returned `id` is stored in collection variables as `activeConversationId` for use in subsequent requests.
Start a conversation with no input (uses defaults).

exec: // Ensure we have a conversation ID
console.warn('No activeConversationId set. Run "Start Conversation" first.');

graphql.variables: {"input": {"conversationId": "{{activeConversationId}}", "content": "Hello, can you summarize the last meeting?", "role": "user"}}

request.description:
Send a user message to an active conversation.

Requires `activeConversationId` to be set (run "Start Conversation" first).

item.name: PII Anonymization
item.description: PII (Personally Identifiable Information) detection and anonymization. Tests the regex-based scrubbing pipeline.

exec: pm.test('PII result has correct structure', function () {
pm.test('PII detected in test input', function () {

graphql.variables: {"text": "Please contact john.doe@example.com or call +45 12345678 for the meeting with CPR 010190-1234."}

request.description:
Check text for PII without permanently modifying it. Returns anonymized version and match statistics.

Detects:
- Email addresses
- Phone numbers
- Danish CPR numbers
- Credit card numbers
- IP addresses

graphql.variables: {"input": {"text": "Customer card 4111111111111111 from IP 192.168.1.100 reported by user@company.dk"}}

request.description: Anonymize PII in text using the mutation endpoint. Supports selective type filtering.

exec: // Email should be redacted (it's in enabled_types)

request.description: Anonymize only specific PII types. Pass `enabledTypes` array to filter which patterns are applied.

exec: pm.test('No PII detected in clean text', function () {
pm.test('Text unchanged when no PII found', function () {

request.description: Negative test: verify clean text passes through without modification.

item.name: PII Scrub Workflow
item.description: Test PII detection accuracy across various input types

graphql.query: query { checkPii(text: "Send report to manager@company.dk") { anonymizedText piiFound matchCount } }

exec: pm.test('Phone number detected as PII', function () {

graphql.query: query { checkPii(text: "Call me at +45 22334455") { anonymizedText piiFound matchCount } }

exec: pm.test('Danish CPR number detected as PII', function () {

graphql.query: query { checkPii(text: "Patient CPR: 010190-1234") { anonymizedText piiFound matchCount } }
query { checkPii(text: "User admin@corp.dk with CPR 150585-4321 called from +45 11223344") { anonymizedText piiFound matchCount } }
